title: Polypodiodes bourretii
intro: Polypodiodes bourretii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Polypodiodes bourretii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusate, or formulated into capsules or tablets, and is also used in herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Polypodiodes bourretii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta.
